================================================================================
CUSTOMERMASTER DATABASE - DATA DICTIONARY
================================================================================
Legacy System: CustomerMaster v3.2.1
Last Updated: 2015-08-14
Migration Status: PENDING to new ERP system

================================================================================
TABLE: tblCustomer
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Purpose: Stores customer master data from the legacy CRM system
Naming Convention: Hungarian notation (str = string, dtm = datetime, bol = boolean)
Primary Key: strCustID (alphanumeric customer code inherited from AS/400 system)

Field Specifications:
--------------------
strCustID (VARCHAR 50, PK)
    - Customer identifier in format C-XXXXX or REG-XXXX
    - Legacy AS/400 customer number
    - Contains letters, numbers, hyphens
    - NOT NULL, UNIQUE

strFullName (VARCHAR 255)
    - Customer's full name (free text field)
    - Format: "FirstName LastName" or "Company Name"
    - NOT NULL

strEmail (VARCHAR 150)
    - Primary email address for contact
    - May contain multiple emails separated by semicolon
    - Optional field (nullable)

strRegion (VARCHAR 100)
    - Sales region or territory
    - Values: "North", "South", "East", "West", "International"
    - Used for sales territory assignment

strCountry (VARCHAR 100)
    - Customer's country
    - Full country name (not ISO code)
    - Example: "United States", "United Kingdom"

dtmCreated (DATETIME)
    - Record creation timestamp
    - Auto-populated by trigger

dtmLastModified (DATETIME)
    - Last modification timestamp
    - Updated by trigger on any field change

bolActive (BIT)
    - Active customer flag
    - 1 = active, 0 = inactive
    - Inactive customers excluded from marketing

cust_id (INT, NULL)
    - NEW: Planned integer surrogate key
    - Migration placeholder for new system
    - Currently NULL for all records

customer_name (VARCHAR 255, NULL)
    - NEW: Standardized naming convention
    - Redundant with strFullName
    - Planned for migration compatibility

txtNotes (TEXT)
    - Free-form notes about customer
    - Used by customer service team
    - Contains migration notes, special handling

================================================================================
TABLE: tblProduct
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Purpose: Product catalog master
Naming Convention: Hungarian notation mixed with standard SQL

Field Specifications:
--------------------
intProdID (INT, PK)
    - Integer product identifier
    - Auto-increment, identity column
    - Used in foreign key references

strSKU (VARCHAR 50, UNIQUE)
    - Stock Keeping Unit
    - Format: Category-Color-Size (e.g., "SHIRT-BLU-L")
    - Must be unique

strName (VARCHAR 255)
    - Product display name
    - E-commerce product title

strDescription (TEXT)
    - Long product description
    - HTML content (stored as-is, no sanitization)

fltPrice (DECIMAL 10,2)
    - Unit selling price
    - Must be > 0
    - Stored with 2 decimal places

intStockQty (INT)
    - Current stock quantity
    - Updated by inventory transactions
    - Can be negative (backorders allowed)

bolActive (BIT)
    - Product availability flag
    - 1 = available for sale
    - 0 = discontinued or out of circulation

strCategory (VARCHAR 100)
    - Product category
    - Hierarchical: "Clothing > Shirts > Casual"

strBrand (VARCHAR 100)
    - Brand or manufacturer name
    - Free text field

dtmIntroduced (DATETIME)
    - Product launch date
    - When product was added to catalog

dtmDiscontinued (DATETIME)
    - Discontinuation date
    - NULL if currently active

Legacy Deprecated Fields:
------------------------
prod_num (VARCHAR 20)
    - OLD: Legacy product number
    - Superseded by strSKU
    - Retained for historical reporting

item_desc (VARCHAR 500)
    - OLD: Truncated description
    - Superseded by strDescription
    - Do not use for new code

unit_cost (VARCHAR 20)
    - OLD: Cost stored as string (BUG!)
    - Should be DECIMAL
    - Contains currency symbols: "$19.99"
    - Requires parsing before calculations

================================================================================
TABLE: vw_SalesOrderHdr
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Purpose: Sales order header records
Note: Despite "vw_" prefix, this is a TABLE, not a view
Legacy quirk: Originally a view, materialized to table in 2010

Field Specifications:
--------------------
lngOrderID (INT, PK)
    - Long integer order ID
    - Identity column, ever-increasing
    - Prefix "lng" from early VB6 days

intCustID (VARCHAR 50, FK)
    - Foreign key to tblCustomer.strCustID
    - Note: int prefix but actually VARCHAR
    - Naming inconsistency from schema evolution

dtmOrderDate (DATETIME)
    - Order creation date/time
    - Set by application, not database default

dtmShipDate (DATETIME)
    - Actual shipment date
    - NULL until shipped

dtmRequiredDate (DATETIME)
    - Customer's requested delivery date
    - Used for fulfillment prioritization

strOrderStatus (VARCHAR 20)
    - Order workflow status
    - Values: "PENDING", "SHIPPED", "CANCELLED"
    - CHECK constraint enforced

fltSubTotal (DECIMAL 12,2)
    - Subtotal of line items (before tax)
    - Calculated from ord_line_item table

fltTaxAmount (DECIMAL 12,2)
    - Tax calculated on order
    - Based on customer's region tax rate

fltTotalAmount (DECIMAL 12,2)
    - Final order total (subtotal + tax + shipping)
    - Includes shipping costs if applicable

strShippingMethod (VARCHAR 50)
    - Shipping carrier/method
    - Examples: "UPS Ground", "FedEx 2Day", "USPS Priority"

strTrackingNumber (VARCHAR 100)
    - Carrier tracking number
    - NULL until shipped

================================================================================
TABLE: ord_line_item
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Purpose: Order line items
Naming Convention: Inconsistent - some abbreviated, some full

Field Specifications:
--------------------
line_id (INT, PK)
    - Line item unique identifier
    - Composite key would be better, but legacy uses surrogate

ord_id (INT, FK)
    - Foreign key to vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID
    - Note: Uses lngOrderID but field named ord_id (inconsistent)

prod_id (INT, FK)
    - Foreign key to tblProduct.intProdID
    - References product master

qty (INT)
    - Quantity ordered
    - Must be positive integer

amt (DECIMAL 10,2)
    - Line item total amount (qty * unit_price)
    - At time of order (snapshotted, not current price)

disc_amt (DECIMAL 10,2)
    - Discount amount applied to this line
    - Can be zero

line_status (VARCHAR 20)
    - Line item status
    - Values: "PENDING", "PICKED", "SHIPPED", "BACKORDERED"

product_code (VARCHAR 50)
    - REDUNDANT: Copy of tblProduct.strSKU
    - Denormalized for reporting performance
    - May be out of sync with master

item_name (VARCHAR 255)
    - REDUNDANT: Copy of product name at order time
    - Snapshotted for historical accuracy
    - Does NOT update if product name changes

================================================================================
TABLE: inv_txn_log
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Purpose: Inventory transaction log
Naming Convention: Heavily abbreviated

Field Specifications:
--------------------
txn_id (INT, PK)
    - Transaction identifier
    - Auto-increment

prod_id (INT, FK)
    - Foreign key to tblProduct.intProdID
    - Product being adjusted

txn_type (VARCHAR 10)
    - Transaction type
    - Values: "IN" (receipt), "OUT" (shipment/adjustment), "ADJ" (correction)
    - CHECK constraint enforced

qty (INT)
    - Quantity change
    - Positive for IN, negative for OUT
    - Can be positive or negative for ADJ

txn_dt (DATETIME)
    - Transaction timestamp
    - DEFAULT GETDATE() in SQL Server

ref_num (VARCHAR 50)
    - Reference number
    - Order number, PO number, or adjustment code

user_id (VARCHAR 50)
    - User who initiated transaction
    - References User.UserName (not enforced by FK)

notes (TEXT)
    - Free-form notes
    - Reason for adjustment, etc.

================================================================================
TABLE: Group (Reserved Word)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Purpose: Product groups/categories
Note: "Group" is a SQL reserved word, requires square brackets

Field Specifications:
--------------------
GroupID (INT, PK)
    - Group identifier
    - Auto-increment

GroupName (VARCHAR 100)
    - Group display name
    - Used for category navigation

ParentGroupID (INT, FK)
    - Self-referencing foreign key
    - NULL for top-level groups
    - Creates hierarchical structure

Description (VARCHAR 500)
    - Group description
    - HTML content

IsActive (BIT)
    - Active flag
    - Inactive groups hidden from UI

================================================================================
TABLE: User (Reserved Word)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Purpose: System users
Note: "User" is a SQL reserved word, requires square brackets

Field Specifications:
--------------------
UserID (INT, PK)
    - User identifier
    - Auto-increment

UserName (VARCHAR 100, UNIQUE)
    - Login username
    - Used throughout system

PasswordHash (VARCHAR 255)
    - Hashed password
    - SHA-256 hash (not salted - security issue!)

UserRole (VARCHAR 50)
    - User role/permission level
    - Values: "ADMIN", "MANAGER", "CLERK"
    - CHECK constraint enforced

Email (VARCHAR 150)
    - User email address
    - For notifications

LastLogin (DATETIME)
    - Last successful login timestamp
    - NULL for new users

CreatedDate (DATETIME)
    - Account creation date
    - DEFAULT GETDATE()

================================================================================
TABLE: tblOrderStatusHistory
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Purpose: Audit log of order status changes
Naming Convention: Verbose (tbl prefix)

Field Specifications:
--------------------
HistoryID (INT, PK)
    - History record identifier
    - Auto-increment

OrderID (INT, FK)
    - Foreign key to vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID

OldStatus (VARCHAR 20)
    - Previous status value
    - NULL for initial status

NewStatus (VARCHAR 20)
    - New status value
    - Current status after change

ChangedByUser (VARCHAR 100)
    - Username who made the change
    - References User.UserName

ChangedDate (DATETIME)
    - Timestamp of status change
    - DEFAULT GETDATE()

ChangeReason (TEXT)
    - Reason for status change
    - Free text (cancellation reasons, etc.)

================================================================================
TABLE: tblShippingCarrier
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Purpose: Shipping carrier master data

Field Specifications:
--------------------
CarrierID (INT, PK)
    - Carrier identifier
    - Auto-increment

CarrierName (VARCHAR 100)
    - Carrier display name
    - Examples: "UPS", "FedEx", "USPS"

CarrierCode (VARCHAR 20, UNIQUE)
    - Carrier code for API integration
    - Examples: "UPS", "FDX", "USPS"

TrackingURL (VARCHAR 500)
    - URL template for tracking
    - Placeholder: {TRACKING_NUM}
    - Example: "https://www.ups.com/track?tracknum={TRACKING_NUM}"

bolActive (BIT)
    - Active carrier flag
    - Inactive carriers not available for new orders

================================================================================
TABLE: tblPayment
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Purpose: Payment transaction records
Security Note: Contains sensitive card data (compliance issue!)

Field Specifications:
--------------------
PaymentID (INT, PK)
    - Payment identifier
    - Auto-increment

OrderID (INT, FK)
    - Foreign key to vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID

PaymentMethod (VARCHAR 50)
    - Payment method type
    - Values: "CREDIT_CARD", "DEBIT", "PAYPAL", "BANK_TRANSFER"
    - CHECK constraint enforced

PaymentDate (DATETIME)
    - Payment processing date
    - When payment was captured

Amount (DECIMAL 12,2)
    - Payment amount
    - Must match order total

PaymentStatus (VARCHAR 20)
    - Payment processing status
    - Values: "PENDING", "COMPLETED", "FAILED", "REFUNDED"

CardNumberText (VARCHAR 100)
    - SECURITY ISSUE: Full card number stored as text!
    - Should be tokenized
    - Contains unencrypted PAN (Primary Account Number)

CardHolderName (VARCHAR 100)
    - Cardholder name
    - As provided by customer

AuthorizationCode (VARCHAR 50)
    - Payment gateway auth code
    - Proof of successful authorization

================================================================================
MIGRATION NOTES:
================================================================================
Known Issues:
1. Hungarian notation inconsistent (intCustID is VARCHAR, lngOrderID is INT)
2. Reserved words used as table names (Group, User) require quoting
3. Inappropriate data types (unit_cost is VARCHAR, should be DECIMAL)
4. Security violations (card numbers stored in plaintext)
5. Denormalized redundant fields (product_code, item_name in line items)
6. Mixed naming conventions (tbl_, vw_, ord_, inv_, no prefix)
7. Abbreviated field names (cust_nm, ord_dt in reports, not in schema)
8. Missing constraints (no CHECK for positive quantities)
9. TEXT fields used for variable-length data (should use VARCHAR(MAX))
10. Bit columns used inconsistently (bol vs BIT vs no suffix)

Recommendations for New Schema:
1. Remove all Hungarian notation prefixes
2. Use consistent naming (snake_case or camelCase)
3. Implement proper data types (DECIMAL for money, INT for IDs)
4. Add proper constraints and indexes
5. Tokenize sensitive payment data
6. Normalize denormalized fields
7. Use views for backward compatibility during migration
8. Rename tables to avoid reserved words
9. Add proper foreign key constraints
10. Implement audit triggers
